@xyz8512 ❤️ 3
People would go to Damascus to buy wootz. Wootz was an excellent steel made in India and transported along the silk road to Damascus. It had a very particular look and people called it 'Damascus steel" but it was not pattern welded steel. What everybody is calling "Damascus" is actually pattern welded steel and that is what Valyrian steel is trying to look like. It's a very common misconception.

This was actually improv! The actor playing Freddy decided that he wouldblow the ship up as a prank. The editor found this so good he decided to keep it in without the directors permission. @greyleblanc ❤️ 7
Lifeguard here to explain the shower rule! Our body's have natural oils (and sweat) and any other sort of oils from hair products, deodorants, lotions, etc... having everyone take a shower helps to rinse those off and improve water quality. It is especially important for pools that receive high patron usage, but its also just overall a good practice! Usually the pool management will require lifeguards to enforce the rule (so dont get mad at lifeguards for doing our jobs, get mad at management 🤷‍♂️😂)

When King Leonidas realized they were being outflanked, he ordered the majority of the Greek allies to retreat. The remaining rear-guard for the final stand consisted of about 1,500 men:
300 Spartans
700 Thespians (who stayed voluntarily)
400 Thebans
Several hundred other allied troops and enslaved servants (helots)
But it doesn't make it look as kewl for a movie.

@BigBobzilla69420 ❤️ 1
My Dad was a Marine for 30 years—he was a cwo 2 the first time he went to Iraq. He was one of those in charge on a really long convoy. He said that at some point, they came across a huge spiny tailed lizard and a bunch of Marines were messing with it and parading it around. He told them to stop f-ing around and to leave the mf behind. He said the convoy went on for another two weeks—and at the end of those two weeks, he caught the same Marines with the lizard because a group of Marines were taking pics of him—the lizard was wearing a sombrero, sunglasses, and had a cigar in its mouth, just chilling, seated in the back of a humvee. The Marines kept him as a mascot and were feeding him along the remainder of the trip lol

@opposed4779 ❤️ 687
This was actually improv! The actor playing Freddy decided that he wouldblow the ship up as a prank. The editor found this so good he decided to keep it in without the directors permission. At the first screening of the movie the director leaned over and told the editor he was fired but, after the crowd went insane and gave a 69 minute round of applause the director took credit and kept the editor on his team.

@RookieMistakeYT ❤️ 120
I’m just a guy that dropped out of college after a year of majoring in Kinesiology but when you make a fist the finger flexors in the forearm contract strongly. Those muscles cross the wrist, so your wrist stabilizers must also contract to prevent the wrist from bending. That tension can “spread” upward through muscular irradiation: the harder you grip, the more the biceps, triceps, shoulder, chest, and upper-back muscles tend to brace.
That extra bracing can help when you need total-body force, such as lifting something heavy. But it can also create unnecessary co-contraction, meaning opposing muscles tighten at the same time. The result is a stiffer arm that may feel slower, heavier, or weaker in a specific movement.
